MINI MINUTES: Appearances (by Zoom):

THE PARTIES STIPULATE TO THE MATTER BEING DECIDED BY THE JUDGE PRO TEM. THE PARTIES AND THE JUDGE PRO TEM APPEARING REMOTELY AUTHORIZE THE CLERK TO SIGN THE FORMAL STIPULATION FORM ON THEIR BEHALF.

PLAINTIFF ELDA AGAVE, LLC's Motion To Compel Depositions Of Defendants Dick Mak And Jesamine Mak, And For Sanctions.

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Feb-05-2020 Set For Trial On Aug-31-20 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel (1) Responses To Form Interrogatories, (2) Responses To Special Interrogatories, (3) Responses To Requests For Production, And (4) Deemed Admissions To Requests For Admission; And, For Sanctions
Off Calendar Jan-30-2020 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Ravi D. Sahae, Esq., Leland Parachini Steinberg Matzger & Melnick LLP, 415-957-1800, appearing for Plaintiff Elda Agave, LLC;
John chow, Esq., Chan chow & Dai, 415-398-8308, appearing for Defendant Dick Mack et al;

Hearing is held. The Court having reviewed the pleadings and having heard oral argument rules as follows: The Court adopts the tentative ruling as modified:

PLAINTIFF ELDA AGAVE, LLC'S Motion To Compel (1) Responses To Form Interrogatories, (2) Responses To Special Interrogatories, (3) Responses To Requests For Production, And (4) Deemed Admissions To Requests For Admission; And, For Sanctions

Granted. Within 10 days, Defendants Dick and Jesamine Mak shall serve verified, Code-complaint responses without objections except privilege to Plaintiff's Form Interrogatories, Set One; Special Interrogatories, Set One; and Requests for Production of Documents, Set One. As to Plaintiff's Requests for Admission (Set One), the genuineness of any documents and the truth of any matters specified in the requests are deemed admitted by Defendants Dick and Jesamine Mak pursuant to C.C.P. sec. 2031.300 without prejudice to Defendant's right to file a motion for relief pursuant to CCP Section 2033.280(c), demonstrating the defendants served, before the hearing, a proposed response to the RFA's that are in substantial compliance with CCP. 2033.220. Plaintiff's request for sanctions against Defendants is granted because Defendants' failure to respond is without substantial justification and Defendants did not seek a protective order. Defendants Dick and Jesamine Mak shall pay to Plaintiff's attorneys Leland, Parachini, Steinberg, Matzger & Melnick, LLP, monetary sanctions in the amount of $1,000, within 30 days. Order is signed.

Judge Pro Tem: Aaron Minnis; Clerk: William Trupek; Not reported. (302/JPT)
